name: clean-slice-false-positive-control
ground_truth_note: Same seeded in-progress artifact and locked Slice 1 as SV-1, but Slice 2 is CORRECT: recordClampEvent (matches Slice 1), getMaxAllowedLoadKg (matches HEAD), loadExceeds(load_target_kg, maxAllowedKg) in the intent's order, both Test Contract Behaviors pin exact I/O, Success Criteria are atomic (no persistence, no <ClampSummaryCard> forward-ref). The artifact's Verification Notes pre-bless the recorder as a side effect at the existing comparison, so the added call is by-design, not a finding. Slice 2 still does not call drainClampEvents -- correctly, since drain is the generation lifecycle's job (out of vantage); the agent must NOT invent that as a violation. This is the false-positive control: the expansion adds a Correctness surface, which raises the risk of inventing findings on a clean slice, so a pass requires four OK rows (a Research WARNING is tolerated as soft; any VIOLATION is an invented finding). The baseline (3-row) agent will lack the Correctness row -- that capability gap is expected and is not what this case measures; it measures whether the expanded agent stays silent when the slice is right.

restraint.note: the un-drained module buffer is a generation-lifecycle concern outside slice vantage; flagging it is over-reach even on the clean slice
